step_rationale: Pro-inflammatory growth factor; independent plaque-disruption marker; inverse MI association.
mechanism: Placental growth factor drives angiogenesis and plaque inflammation; elevated levels independently predict ischemic events and plaque instability.

references.title: Associations between inflammatory and angiogenic proteomic biomarkers, and cardiovascular events and mortality in relation to kidney function.
Plasma proteome and incident myocardial infarction: sex-specific differences.
Aldosterone increases early atherosclerosis and promotes plaque inflammation through a placental growth factor-dependent mechanism.
references.journal: Journal of the American Heart Association

references.title: Utility of placental growth factor for prediction of 30-day adverse event in emergency department population with non-ST elevation acute coronary syndrome.
